concerns.text: The ablation study design in FR-005 and SC-002 proposes masking 'bond types' to measure their contribution. However, in a standard 2D molecular graph (SMILES-derived), bond types are definitionally required to construct the adjacency matrix and edge features. If bond types are masked (set to a uniform value), the graph topology effectively collapses or becomes chemically meaningless, making the resulting MAE increase a trivial consequence of destroying the input structure rather than an empirical finding about the 'predictive signal' of bond types. This risks a tautological validation where the model fails because the input is broken, not because the feature was unimportant. The spec needs a methodologically sound ablation strategy (e.g., feature perturbation, attention masking, or using a model architecture that explicitly separates these inputs) that preserves graph validity while isolating the feature's contribution.

SC-004 claims to measure 'generalization capability' by comparing performance on 'test set (unseen scaffolds)' vs 'validation set (seen scaffolds)'. This is a category error in statistical validation. The validation set is part of the training loop (used for hyperparameter tuning/early stopping) and is therefore 'seen' by the model selection process. Comparing test performance to validation performance does not measure generalization to new chemical space; it measures the gap between the held-out tuning set and the final held-out test set. True generalization to unseen scaffolds is measured ONLY by the test set performance against the training set performance (or against a baseline). The proposed comparison is statistically invalid for the claimed purpose.

The spec requires comparing the GNN MAE against a 'DFTB baseline' (FR-006, SC-001). DFTB (Density Functional Tight Binding) is a semi-empirical quantum mechanical method that requires 3D atomic coordinates to compute energies. The spec's core hypothesis (Constitution Principle VI) and preprocessing (FR-002) explicitly restrict the GNN to 2D graphs (SMILES) and prohibit 3D coordinates unless justified by ablation. If the DFTB baseline is run on 3D coordinates (which it must be to function), the comparison is between a 2D-only model and a 3D-dependent method. This is an apples-to-oranges comparison that confounds 'structural representation' with 'methodological accuracy'. The spec must clarify if the DFTB baseline will be run on the same 2D-derived 3D conformers (introducing a confounding variable of conformer generation quality) or if a different 2D-compatible baseline is intended.
